metadata.title:
MASTER BUILDERS ASSOCIATION


LET THE CAT OUT OF THE BAG

pages.raw_text:
every description in the city ready
to take the places Of the unionists
when the lookout was declared. Many
Of these men are now In the city
awaiting the word. All seems to
have been working as the as0ocia-
tion wanted up to this time when IL
was found the stumbling block for
their plans would be the masons.


By the unwilling advertising for
these masons and bricklayers they
laid their plans bare to the building
body today claim they now have an
insight into the Vaccinations Of the
association and will at once take
steps in the matter.


Acting Secretary Weiser Of the
trades council said this morning:


There has been much talk regard
ing this lockout, but heretofore We
have laughed at the Master Builders
association. The unions are so strono
in the city that we knew 1t would
be absolutely impossible to declare a
lockout. or rather make a lockout eff
fective. The members of the associ-
alton want to claim that a lockout
is already in effect. 1t don't looh
like It. There are about 10 hod-
carriers in the city, and there are not
a dozen of them who are not work.
ing on the buildings now being erect
ed. The tip is now out and the as
ISOLATION will find 1t the hardest thing
in the world to fight us. We are just
as strong as ever and all the unions
are growing in strength"
